title: Apply for the Massachusetts Rental Voucher Program (MRVP) | Mass.gov
content:
# Apply for the Massachusetts Rental Voucher Program (MRVP) | Mass.gov

MRVP is a state program offering rental assistance in the form of vouchers to low-income families (similar to Section 8). The vouchers take 2 forms: tenant-based (mobile) or project-based.

## Types of Vouchers and What They Cover

Like Section 8, with MRVP you can apply for a rental voucher to help you pay rent. There are 2 types of vouchers:

- **Tenant-based or mobile vouchers**: Use them for any housing unit that meets the standards of the state sanitary code. The amount is limited based on your voucher size and zip code.
- **Project-based vouchers**: Tied to specific housing units. The owner rents these units to you, and you receive voucher payments for as long as you continue to live in the unit.

For either voucher, you will pay at least 30% of your net monthly income towards rent. Even if you are eligible, you will be placed on a waiting list because of high demand.

## Eligibility

Individuals, families, people with disabilities and elders are all eligible for MRVP. The income limit for eligibility for MRVP is 80% of Area Median Income.

You may be eligible for homeless priority, which speeds up the application process. If you qualify, you'll receive an email, phone call, and letter from the Centralized Screening Office (Archipelago Strategies Group/ASG).

## How to Apply

### Online
You can apply for MRVP and check the status of your application through Common Housing Application for Massachusetts Programs (CHAMP). You may also be eligible for the Alternative Housing Voucher Program (AHVP).

### In Person
You can also fill out a paper application (for both MRVP and AHVP) and submit it to your local housing agency.

## Next Steps

After you apply, you will be placed on the waiting lists that you choose. Demand for vouchers is very high, so the wait may be lengthy. Keep your contact information updated so housing agencies can reach you.

When you get near the top of a housing agency's waitlist, they will contact you to review your eligibility. If eligible, they'll explain details of the program and next steps.

## Contact

- **Phone:** 617-573-1250 (M-F 8:45am - 5:00pm)
- **Email:** EOHLCmrvp@mass.gov
- **Address:** 100 Cambridge Street, Suite 300, Boston, MA 02114
